title: spiranthes biflora
intro: Spiranthes biflora, also known as the yellow ladies' tresses, is a type of orchid native to North America, characterized by its yellow flowers that resemble a spiral or twisted shape. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Spiranthes biflora have been found to have various health benefits, including treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying the flowers and leaves, and can be consumed as a tea or used topically as a poultice or ointment.
